description_long: This object is a large, dark stone sculpture depicting two seated human figures, likely of ancient Egyptian origin, carved from a single block. Both figures are shown with stylized, shoulder-length hair and wear simple garments, sitting side by side on a high-backed bench or throne. The male figure on the left has one arm crossed over his chest, while the female figure on the right rests her hands on her lap. The sides and front of the seat feature vertical columns of hieroglyphic inscriptions, adding to the object's historical and decorative significance. The surface is mostly smooth but shows subtle signs of weathering and age, with some minor chipping and wear along the edges. The overall form is solid and monolithic, making it a formidable obstacle or barrier in any urban setting.
description: A large, dark stone sculpture of two seated figures with hieroglyphic inscriptions, carved in a monolithic style. The surface is smooth with minor weathering and chipping.
description_view_0: Front view showing both figures seated side by side, with clear visibility of their faces, hands, and the hieroglyphic inscriptions on the front of the seat.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the figures and the detailed hieroglyphic inscriptions running vertically along the side of the seat.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid, unadorned back of the sculpture, with the figures' heads and backs visible but no inscriptions or details.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile of the figures and the hieroglyphic inscriptions on the right side of the seat, with the male figure's crossed arm more prominent.
attribute_location: Typically found at the entrance to historical sites, museums, or as a decorative barrier in urban plazas and parks, often positioned near pathways or building entrances.
attribute_installation_orientation: Fully above ground, upright, and flush with the surrounding surface.
attribute_wear_patterns: Minor chipping and smoothing along the edges and corners, likely caused by weathering, occasional contact with pedestrians, and environmental exposure. Some surface discoloration may be present due to age and outdoor placement.
attribute_historical_markings: Vertical columns of hieroglyphic inscriptions are present on the front and both sides of the seat, carved in a traditional ancient Egyptian style.
